other_observers: Steve Brown; none that I know of
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch Headquarters pond area
description: A brownish-olive backed Catharus thrush. Very plain gray face with a slight eyering mostly posteriorly. The breast was heavily spotted with blackish spots on a pale grayish-white breast.  The basal half of the mandible was a yellow-flesh colorBehaviors: Hopping on the ground and in low shrubsCall: A burry Weeer call and a soft gurgling rich thrush song, impossible to put into words.Plumage: Adult Definitive
photographer: Photographer: Brian P Gibbons, Brian P Gibbons
